title: ai-paralegal citation gate verifies existence, not format — silent pass-through gap
content: The citations gate verifyCitations() (src/lib/citations/verify.ts) — the only producer of branded VerifiedLegalText (CULTURE.md §3) — verifies that cites EXIST (CourtListener lookup) but does NOT validate FORMAT. Its detection is gated entirely by CITATION_REGEX via parseCitations(). A citation that misses the strict regex (e.g. '410 US 113' without periods, '410 U.S. 113 at 115', transposed reporter) is neither 'found' nor 'failed' and is silently branded and shown to the user. Statutes/procedural rules (FRCP, U.S.C. §, state codes) explicitly pass through unchecked (see discovery/actions.ts comment). CitationError has only verification_failed + lookup_unavailable — no vocabulary for 'present but malformed'. This is the gap ADR-0004 addresses: a format-validation stage runs BEFORE existence lookup, distinct from existence failures. Future citation work should preserve this format-vs-existence distinction and keep the gate as the sole VerifiedLegalText producer.

title: ai-paralegal ADR log conventions and prior decisions
content: ai-paralegal keeps an ADR decision log at docs/adr/ (ADR-NNNN-slug.md). Format: title, Status/Date/Driver/Author/Builds-on header, then Context (evidence with file:line citations) / Decision / Consequences (Positive + Negative/risks + UNVERIFIED notes) / Task breakdown with story points. Prior ADRs: ADR-0001 (credential-access audit log — adopt structured-event-log not SQLite/event-bus), ADR-0002 (architecture overview — Next.js 16 + React 19, two choke-points: callAI.ts and verifyCitations), ADR-0003 (prompt-injection/exfiltration defense at AI choke-point). New ADRs should reference and build on these, not contradict them. Persona memory was empty before this run — these ADRs live in the repo, not persona memory.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-26 22:38 UTC
ai-paralegal: citation gate sends full privileged document to third-party CourtListener API
content: SECURITY/CONFIDENTIALITY finding (file:line signature: src/lib/citations/verify.ts → runCourtListenerLookup, the fetch POST body JSON.stringify({ text })). The citation gate POSTs the ENTIRE AI-drafted document (raw) — plaintiff name, medical chronology, settlement valuation, privileged matter facts — to courtlistener.com on every verification when COURTLISTENER_API_TOKEN is set. This is an attorney-client-privilege / data-egress surface not covered by ADR-0003 (which hardened the AI choke-point only). Recommended fix: send only citation-shaped spans (parseCitations gives start/end) or gate live lookup behind per-tenant opt-in + DPA. This finding is OPEN and was the primary security driver of a REQUEST_CHANGES verdict on the ADR-0004 baseline review. Not yet in ADR-0004 scope — should be added or given a sibling security ADR. Do not re-flag as resolved until span-only transmission or opt-in gating lands.

title: ai-paralegal: discovery action mishandles lookup_unavailable (folds into model_error)
content: Correctness finding (signature: src/features/discovery/actions.ts → verifyResult error branch 'lookup_unavailable — treat as model_error for now'). Discovery's draftRfaResponses collapses a CourtListener outage into reason 'model_error'; DraftRfaResponsesResult lacks the distinct 'lookup_unavailable' reason that demand-letter/actions.ts already has, and DiscoveryResponsePanel.tsx:221 only handles model_error. Must be reconciled to parity BEFORE ADR-0004 task 6 threads a third format_invalid branch through the same union. Medium severity. The two citation consumers (demand-letter vs discovery) diverge in error handling — verify genuine parity when adding format_invalid, don't just copy one side. OPEN.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-26 22:41 UTC
ai-paralegal: NO real auth — getDevTenantContext() grants attorney sign-off on every request
content: HIGH auth finding (signature: src/lib/tenant.ts:55-63 getDevTenantContext, consumed in demand-letter/actions.ts:49,152, discovery/actions.ts:135, medical-records/actions.ts:91,159,303, and all app/**/page.tsx). Every server action resolves identity from a hardcoded dev context (tenant 'suarez-law', attorney 'atty-suarez' WITH sign-off authority). No session/auth check exists. If deployed, any unauthenticated caller can draft AND sign legal artifacts as a real attorney, and tenant isolation collapses to one tenant. Escalates to CRITICAL in production. Fix: real getServerSession() resolver + startup guard that throws if NODE_ENV=production and dev seam active. OPEN — first scan 2026-05-27. Do not mark fixed until a real auth provider replaces the dev seam.

title: ai-paralegal: dependency + secret baseline (2026-05-27 first scan)
content: Dependency audit (npm audit): 2 MODERATE — postcss <8.5.10 XSS (CWE-79, CVSS 6.1, GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93) pulled transitively via next. Fix = pin postcss>=8.5.10 via package overrides; do NOT run `npm audit fix --force` (it tries to downgrade next to 9.3.3, a destructive rollback). Secret: a live AIza-format GEMINI_API_KEY sits in .env.local plaintext but is gitignored and verified never committed (git log -S empty) — rotate as precaution, not a VCS leak. Also corroborated 2 prior code-review findings independently: (1) verify.ts:142 full-document egress to CourtListener; (2) verify.ts:240-251 silent pass-through of zero-cite docs. All OPEN.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-26 22:44 UTC
ai-paralegal release decision: v0.2.0 (first tagged release, MINOR)
content: On 2026-05-27 cut the FIRST tagged release of ai-paralegal: bumped package.json 0.1.0 → 0.2.0 (MINOR), created CHANGELOG.md (Keep a Changelog style) and annotated tag v0.2.0 on commit 77d069e. Triggering merge: 73b4573 'Upgrade Next.js dependencies'. Rationale: pre-1.0 (0.x) SemVer convention — breaking changes increment the MINOR, backward-compatible work increments PATCH. The merge was a breaking PLATFORM migration (next 14→16, react 18→19, tailwindcss 3→4, typescript 5→6, eslint 9→10) requiring code adaptation (postcss.config.mjs→@tailwindcss/postcss, tsconfig, globals.css, DraftPanel useEffect removal), so MINOR not PATCH; not 1.0 GA so not MAJOR. CONSISTENCY RULE for future releases: this project is private/pre-1.0 — use 0.x rules (breaking→minor, feature/fix→patch) until an explicit 1.0.0 GA decision. Push to remote + GitHub release publish were left for human approval, not auto-shipped.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-26 22:48 UTC
README stack section drifts on platform-migration releases
content: On the v0.2.0 release, the Release Manager keeps CHANGELOG.md current as part of the release commit, but README.md is NOT auto-updated and goes stale. The README 'What's in this repo' line and 'Tech stack' table hard-code framework versions (Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind, ESLint), so any dependency-upgrade release leaves them wrong. Docs Steward's main value on release events is reconciling these README version references against package.json — the changelog is usually already handled upstream.
